author: Kawê Cezar
description: Automatically apply specific properties to new notes based on their folder with a simple key-value UI. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Kawê Cezar
manifest.description: Automatically apply specific properties to new notes based on their folder with a simple key-value UI.
manifest.name: Folder Auto Properties

name: Folder Auto Properties

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
# Release v2.0.4

## 🚀 New Features

### Counter Property for Auto-Incrementing Files
- **Auto-numbering**: Files can now automatically get sequential numbers (e.g., "Lesson 1", "Lesson 2", "Lesson 3")
- **Custom prefixes**: Set your own prefix like "Chapter", "Aula", "Meeting", etc.
- **Separator toggle**: Choose to add " - " after the number (e.g., "Lesson 1 - ") or just the number
- **Smart increment**: Always increments to the next available number, even if you manually type a number
- **Works with other plugins**: Compatible with plugins that create notes with custom names (like YouTube importers)

### Start File with Separator
- **Optional `---` at file start**: Enable to automatically add a YAML frontmatter separator at the beginning of new files

### Enhanced UI/UX
- **Better modal design**: Completely redesigned rule configuration modal with sections and improved layout
- **Property type badges**: Visual badges showing property types (Text, Tags, Checkbox, Counter, etc.)
- **Collapsible sub-rules**: Parent rules with sub-rules are now collapsed by default for cleaner organization
- **Animated cards**: Smooth animations for rule cards with staggered entrance effects
- **Empty state indicators**: Helpful messages when no properties or rules are defined
- **Default key names**: Smart defaults for property keys based on type (e.g., "checkbox" for checkbox type)

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Critical Fixes
- **Fixed counter reprocessing on restart**: Files no longer get re-numbered when Obsidian restarts
- **Fixed property re-adding on restart**: Deleted properties won't be re-added when reopening Obsidian
- **Counter auto-increment collision**: If you type "Lesson 1" and it already exists, it will now correctly increment to "Lesson 2"
- **Counter with custom names**: Counter now works even when you type a custom filename (e.g., "My Topic" becomes "Lesson 5 - My Topic")

### Type Safety Improvements
- Fixed unsafe type assignments flagged by Obsidian review bot
- Better error handling throughout the codebase

## 📝 Notes
- **New files only**: Rules only apply to newly created files, not existing ones
- **Backward compatible**: All existing rules will continue to work
- **Minimum Obsidian version**: 1.12.7

## 🎯 How to Use
1. Right-click a folder → "Add folder auto property rule"
2. Configure your properties in the new modal
3. Choose "Counter" type for auto-numbering
4. New files in that folder will automatically get the properties and counter numbers
releases.name: Folder Auto Properties 2.0.4
